key_items: Bitcoin ETFs record 6-day loss streak with $1.55B net outflows; BTC dives below $75K
BlackRock and early Bitcoin whales selling significant holdings; demand metrics hit 2026 lows
Fed minutes signal rate-hike risks, not cuts; macro headwinds intensify with PCE inflation data pending
reasoning: Multiple institutional sellers (BlackRock $1B sale, Trump Media 2,650 BTC transfer, Satoshi-era whale $203M exit) combined with persistent ETF outflows totaling $1.55B over six days create substantial supply pressure. Weakening on-chain demand metrics, Fed rate-hike concerns, and liquidation risk near $75K resistance establish a bearish near-term setup despite some bullish infrastructure developments (Tether-Georgia stablecoin, Fed settlement rails).
risks: Cascading liquidations below $75K could trigger a swift drop to $70K support, exacerbated by low trading demand and potential PCE inflation data volatility on Thursday.
